name: Small Business Lending Center
description_short: U.S. Small Business Administration Houston District Office provides free counseling, funding guidance, and business resources to entrepreneurs across 32 counties in southeastern Texas.
description_long:
The Houston District Office is part of the U.S. Small Business Administration, a federal government agency dedicated to supporting small business development. Established to serve entrepreneurs in southeastern Texas, the office operates from Houston with a team led by District Director Mark Winchester and Deputy District Director Winston Labbé. The office is funded by the federal government and operates as a public resource rather than a commercial lending entity.

The Houston District provides comprehensive small business support including SBA funding program guidance, business counseling, federal contracting certifications, and disaster recovery assistance. The office connects entrepreneurs to partner organizations, lenders, and community groups that support business success. They offer guidance on business registration, help accessing state and federal resources, and facilitate connections to lenders in the area. The office also hosts educational events covering topics like entrepreneurship basics and business marketing skills.

What distinguishes this office is its no-cost, government-backed approach to small business support and its regional focus on 32 counties across southeastern Texas including Harris County (Houston), Galveston, Fort Bend, and surrounding areas. The office maintains direct relationships with both public and private sector partners, creating a network rather than offering direct lending. Their success stories showcase real entrepreneurs who received support, from 3D printing innovators to minority-owned accounting firms.

The primary limitation is that this is a guidance and referral service, not a direct lender—applicants must work with recommended lenders for actual financing. Services are available by appointment only during standard business hours (Monday-Friday, 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.), and the office is closed on federal holidays. Geographic coverage, while extensive, is limited to the Houston district's 32-county service area.
pros: No-cost services funded by federal government—no hidden fees or charges
Access to SBA loan programs and funding guidance directly from federal staff
Serves 32 counties across southeastern Texas with physical office in Houston
Provides federal contracting certification support for qualifying small businesses
Connects entrepreneurs to vetted partner lenders and community organizations
Hosts free educational events on entrepreneurship, marketing, and business growth
Offers disaster recovery assistance for business owners affected by emergencies
cons: Does not directly lend money—operates as referral and advisory service only
Limited to 32-county Houston district area; cannot serve businesses outside this region
Federal office hours (closed weekends, federal holidays, after 4:30 p.m.) may not suit all schedules
Relies on referral partners for actual funding; cannot guarantee lender approval
best_for: Early-stage entrepreneurs in southeastern Texas seeking free business guidance and SBA loan information
Small business owners qualifying for federal contracting opportunities who need certification support
Minority-owned and women-owned business enterprises seeking federal resources and networking
Businesses in 32-county Houston service area affected by disasters seeking recovery assistance
diagnosis: Best for entrepreneurs in the Houston 32-county region (including Harris, Fort Bend, Galveston, and surrounding counties) who need free government-backed guidance on SBA loans, business startup, or federal contracting. Main caveat: this is a federal advisory office, not a lender—actual loan approval requires application to partner lenders they refer, and services require advance appointments.
services: SBA funding program guidance and loan information
Federal contracting certifications (8(a), HUBZone, WOSB, etc.)
Referrals to partner lenders and financial institutions
Connections to community business support organizations
Educational workshops and webinars on entrepreneurship
State and federal business resource navigation
Email alerts with free small business tips and training information
Success story resources from funded businesses
